evals.expected_output: Should output 'Core message: present' and 'Status: READY', then produce challenges that reference the core message about infrastructure and obsession. Should include Phase 2 with evidence/debate.

assertions.text: Output contains 'Core message' status line with present/missing
Output contains 'Status' line with READY/BLOCKED
At least one challenge references the core message text
Output contains Phase 2 section with evidence or debate
Output ends with 'The lesson survives if...' statement
Output contains Credibility evaluation

evals.prompt: /challenge on a lesson file where the '> Core message' line has been removed
evals.expected_output: Should output 'Core message: missing' and 'Status: BLOCKED'. Should NOT produce challenge analysis. Should instruct user to run /think-through first.

assertions.text: Output contains BLOCKED or HALTED or 'unable to proceed'
Output does NOT contain 'Challenge 1' or '## Challenge' style headers
Output mentions /think-through as the next step

evals.expected_output: Should read the Substack draft cold (no scaffold reference). Should produce numbered line edits with specific text quotes. Should generate SEO fields (title, description, slug, tags). Should NOT reference the scaffold file.

assertions.text: Output contains numbered feedback items with quoted text
Output contains SEO Title field (60 chars or less)
Output contains SEO Description field
Output contains URL Slug field
Output does NOT reference '05-the-infrastructure-gap.md' (scaffold file)
Output does NOT reference 'scaffold' as a noun

evals.expected_output: Should output Core message status. Should produce a Substack draft. Should include a ## Cut List section with [Scaffold-only] and [Content-cut] tags. Should ask for approval before /copyedit.

assertions.text: Output contains Core message status (present/missing)
Output contains '## Cut List' or equivalent section
At least one cut tagged [Scaffold-only]
Output mentions approval or waiting before /copyedit
Output checks opening against Simple + Unexpected

evals.expected_output: Should produce structured critique. Should end with 3 questions. Should evaluate hook (Unexpected) and Concrete moment. Should help state/verify core message.

assertions.text: Output contains 3 specific questions for the author
Output evaluates whether the lesson is Unexpected enough to hook
Output identifies a Concrete moment
Output references or helps refine the core message

evals.expected_output: Should spawn 4 agents (Skeptic, Outside Reader, PM Peer, Leadership Expert). Each should reference the core message. Synthesis should have top 5 changes tagged as patch/restructure/rethink.

assertions.text: Output contains feedback from 4 distinct agents
Output contains a Leadership Expert section
Synthesis contains top 5 changes
At least one change is tagged (patch, restructure, or rethink)
Core message is referenced in agent evaluations
